puzzle.title: The Soul Behind the Idol
puzzle.intro: In the shadow of a great exodus from tyranny, this mysterious figure waited in the wilderness. While the leader of a chosen people ascended a sacred mount for divine communion, a great trial descended upon those left behind. This individual seized the moment to reshape the beliefs of an entire community. When the leader returned with truth, a unique and eternal punishment was decreed. Can you unravel who this controversial and cunning soul was?
clues: I traveled with a people who had been freed from the grip of a powerful oppressor through miraculous signs.
When their leader went up to the mountain to receive guidance, I waited among those who remained behind.
I gathered precious metal from their ornaments and fashioned it into something that produced a sound.
Through this creation, I convinced an entire generation that they had found their god in the wilderness.
When the leader returned, I was singled out and asked what drove me to such deception.
My punishment in this world was that no one would touch me or come near, a mark of shame I bear forever.

identity.label: Who Am I?
options: The Samiri
Prophet Musa
Pharaoh's magicians
Haman, minister of Pharaoh
Iblis, the rejected one

trial.label: Trial/Event
options: The making of the golden calf
The confrontation with Pharaoh
The construction of a great ark
The refusal to bow before Adam
The destruction of the cities of Lut
trial.answer: The making of the golden calf

location.label: Key Element
options: A golden calf idol
A staff that turned into a serpent
A she-camel that emerged from rock
An ark built upon a mountain
A throne carried upon the wind

outcome.label: Outcome
options: Condemned to be untouchable, forever saying 'no contact'
Drowned alongside the tyrant and his army
Cast into a fire that became cool and peaceful
Struck with blindness for refusing truth
Abandoned in a pit by jealous kin
outcome.answer: Condemned to be untouchable, forever saying 'no contact'

puzzle.arabic: قَالَ فَٱذْهَبْ فَإِنَّ لَكَ فِى ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ أَن تَقُولَ لَا مِسَاسَ ۖ وَإِنَّ لَكَ مَوْعِدًۭا لَّن تُخْلَفَهُۥ ۖ وَٱنظُرْ إِلَىٰٓ إِلَـٰهِكَ ٱلَّذِى ظَلْتَ عَلَيْهِ عَاكِفًۭا ۖ لَّنُحَرِّقَنَّهُۥ ثُمَّ لَنَنسِفَنَّهُۥ فِى ٱلْيَمِّ نَسْفًا
puzzle.verse: [Moses] said, "Then go. And indeed, it is [decreed] for you in [this] life to say, 'No contact.' And indeed, you have an appointment [in the Hereafter] you will not fail to keep. And look at your 'god' to which you remained devoted. We will surely burn it and blow it [i.e., its ashes] into the sea with a blast. (20:97)
